mundo.titulo: Man who allegedly sold chemical linked to 73 British deaths will not be tried in UK
mundo.resumen: The substance said to have been sold by Kenneth Law is linked to the deaths of 73 British people.

mundo.titulo: Exploding rocket casts doubts over Nasa's Moon plans
mundo.resumen: Explosion of Blue Origin rocket is a setback for the company and for Nasa's Moon plans.

mundo.titulo: US and Iran 'very close' to deal but 'not there yet', Vance says
mundo.resumen: US officials earlier told the BBC that the framework of a ceasefire extension deal had been agreed, pending the approval of Trump and Iran's leadership.

mundo.titulo: Top UK chefs call for 10% VAT cut for pubs and restaurants
mundo.resumen: Tom Kerridge, Yotam Ottolenghi, Ravneet Gill and Simon Rogan told BBC Newsnight VAT should be slashed to 10% to ease mounting pressure on the hospitality industry.

mundo.titulo: No second retrial for men accused of airport brawl
mundo.resumen: Muhammad Amaad and Mohammed Fahir Amaaz denied assaulting a Greater Manchester Police officer in 2024.

mundo.titulo: Child among three dead in tower block fall
mundo.resumen: A man, a woman and a child were pronounced dead at the scene in Elephant and Castle.

negocios.titulo: Only three-quarters of first class mail delivered on time

negocios.titulo: Opportunities shrinking for too many young people, says major report on 'lost generation'
negocios.resumen: A report warns the number of 16 to 24-year-olds out of work, education or training is set to rise to 1.25 million by 2031.

negocios.titulo: 'I've applied for more than 400 roles' - how young people are facing the job shortage
negocios.resumen: The BBC has been hearing from young people who are struggling to find work about how they are tackling the challenge.

negocios.titulo: US government prepares to print $250 note featuring Trump's face
negocios.resumen: Federal law bars printing images of living people on US currency, but Trump allies in Congress are moving to make an exception.

negocios.titulo: Humanoid robots 'the future' of car making, says BMW

tecnologia.resumen: South Korean chip startup XCENA is betting that AI's real bottleneck is not compute, but memory.
